relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job asks why the days of justice are hidden from those who know Shaddai.
Boundary markers are moved and flocks seized.
Orphans and widows are stripped of support.
The poor are pushed off the way and forced into hiding.
They search the wilderness for bread like wild donkeys.
They harvest in fields that are not theirs and glean from the wicked.
They sleep exposed to cold.
Mountain rain soaks them, and rock becomes their shelter.
The vulnerable are taken as pledges.
The hungry carry grain while lacking covering.
They press oil and tread wine yet remain thirsty.
The city groans, the wounded cry out, and Job sees divine delay.
There are rebels against light who refuse its paths.
Violence rises early against the poor and needy.
Hidden desire waits for darkness and covers its face.
The night becomes the workplace of those who do not know light.
Deep darkness is morning to them.
Their portion is unstable on the waters and cursed in the earth.
Drought steals snow-water as Sheol receives the sinner.
Memory fades, and injustice is finally broken like a tree.
The barren and the widow are exploited.
Yet the powerful still rise and unsettle life.
Security is given for a time, while the Divine watches their ways.
They rise briefly, then are gathered like all others.
Job challenges anyone to prove this observation false.
